blog.page.title: Latest articles
blog.page.subtitle: Insights on UX, frontend, and performance.
blog.ux.lead: A beautiful website is not enough. If users can’t find what they need in seconds, they leave. UX is not decoration: it is how it works.
blog.ux.p1: In digital products, tolerance for friction is zero. A one-second delay can cut conversions by 7%. Here are the pillars of modern UX.
blog.ux.p2: As Steve Krug notes, every moment of hesitation has a cost: if users must wonder whether something is clickable, trust and conversion drop.
blog.ux.li1: Clear typographic hierarchy across headings, subheadings, and body.
blog.ux.li2: Intentional whitespace to improve readability and focus.
blog.ux.li3: Consistent color system for CTAs and interactive elements.
blog.ux.p3: Effective design is inclusive: building for accessibility improves outcomes for everyone and reduces friction.
blog.ux.li5: Provide descriptive alternative text for images.
blog.ux.h2_3: 3. Micro-interactions: the devil is in the details
blog.ux.p4: Micro-interactions are small feedback moments that make interfaces feel alive and guide the user.
blog.ux.quote: Micro-interactions are the difference between a product that works and one that users love.
blog.react.lead: React is no longer just a UI library: it is an ecosystem. In 2026 the hybrid Server/Client model reshaped performance and DX.
blog.react.p1: If you are starting today, forget the 2020 tutorials: with React Server Components and modern Next.js, the paradigm has changed.
blog.react.p2: The key challenge is understanding where code runs: server or client.
blog.react.li1: useActionState: manage the state of Server Actions.
blog.react.li2: useOptimistic: update the UI before the server response.
blog.react.li3: use: API to read Context and Promises conditionally.
blog.react.quote: In 2026, productivity is measured in safe shipped features, not lines of code.
blog.react.tip: Pro tip: build a clone of a real app (e.g., Spotify or Trello) to face real state-management problems.
blog.frameworks.lead: The "CSS War" is over: both won. But choosing Bootstrap or Tailwind today is a matter of architecture.
blog.frameworks.p1: On one side, ready-made accessible components; on the other, utilities that give total control. Let’s compare.
blog.frameworks.p2: Ideal for MVPs and dashboards when you need speed and consistency.
blog.frameworks.p3: Perfect for consumer products with strong branding and custom design.
blog.frameworks.verdict: Verdict: choose Bootstrap to ship fast. Choose Tailwind for a custom, pixel-perfect experience.
blog.share2: Did you enjoy the article? Let’s talk!
